# Quantum Inertial Sensing for Navigation, Exploration & Security
![](https://infleqtion.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/Website_Backgrounds_1920x800px_Dark_bg_tex_right.webp)
## Unprecedented Accuracy. Reliable Performance. No GPS Required.
Infleqtion’s quantum inertial sensing technology is inherently stable and accurate over long time-scales because atoms are identical with unchanging properties. This almost eliminates bias errors and drift—critical limitations in conventional inertial sensors such as gravimeters, accelerometers, or gyroscopes.
  * Ultra-high precision gravity mapping for national security and geo-exploration and earth monitoring
  * Long-duration GPS-independent navigation for aerospace and defense, providing resilience and autonomy
  * Absolute measurements reduce the need for calibration and re-calibration, improving up-time


![Grace gravity shifts](https://infleqtion.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/gravitational-anomalies.png)Source: [Grace](https://www2.csr.utexas.edu/grace/gravity/)
![](https://infleqtion.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/Website_Backgrounds_1920x800px_Light_bg_tex_left.webp)
## Infleqtion Inertial Sensing Differentiators
### Core Advantages
  * Miniaturized ultra-high vacuum (UHV) technology at the core of our inertial sensors
  * Integrated photonic circuits (PICs) for robust, compact laser systems
  * Advanced use of [Bose-Einstein Condensates (BECs)](https://infleqtion.com/what-is-a-bose-einstein-condensate/) for new sensor capabilities
  * High-bandwidth sensing for dynamic applications


### The Infleqtion Edge
  * Expertise in atom interferometry delivering ultra-precise measurements
  * Decades-long track record in quantum research and deployment
  * Leveraging hardware and software development from Infleqtion’s range of quantum technology disciplines


![](https://infleqtion.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/Website_Backgrounds_1920x800px_White_bg_tex_right.webp)
## Mission-Critical Inertial Sensing Applications
![national security](https://infleqtion.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/Infleqtion-Icons_National-Security.png)
**National Security & Defense**  
Precise positioning in GPS-denied environments for ships, aircraft, and autonomous operations.
![space](https://infleqtion.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/Infleqtion-Icons_Space-Frontier.png)
**Space & Frontier**  
Gravity mapping for planetary exploration and advanced aerospace missions.
![energy](https://infleqtion.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/Infleqtion-Icons_Energy-and-Resource.png)
**Energy & Resource Exploration**  
Identify subsurface resources with unmatched accuracy for energy and mining sectors.
![](https://infleqtion.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/Website_Backgrounds_1920x800px_Dark_bg_tex_left.webp)
![NASA logo](https://infleqtion.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/Nasa-White-Crop-1024x283.png)
![UK Royal Navy logo](https://infleqtion.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/royal-navy-white-crop.png)
## Collaborating With Global Leaders
Infleqtion is proud to partner with world-class organizations on first-of-their-kind quantum inertial sensing missions:
  * **NASA Cold Atom Lab:** A 12-year collaboration advancing ultra-cold atom science in space. [Read more.](https://www.jpl.nasa.gov/missions/cold-atom-laboratory-cal/)
  * **NASA Quantum Gravity Gradiometer Pathfinder (QGGPf):** Developing a pioneering space-based quantum sensor for Earth’s gravity mapping. [Read more.](https://www.jpl.nasa.gov/news/nasa-aims-to-fly-first-quantum-sensor-for-gravity-measurements/)
  * **QNAV Project:** Partnering with the UK Royal Navy to advance quantum navigation technology. [Read more.](https://www.datacenterdynamics.com/en/news/infleqtion-to-deploy-quantum-computing-lab-aboard-uk-royal-navy-vessel/)


![](https://infleqtion.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/Website_Backgrounds_1920x800px_White_bg.webp)

[Quantum Insider: UK QNAV Project](https://thequantuminsider.com/2024/08/12/infleqtion-uk-successfully-completes-first-phase-of-quantum-navigation-project/)
![](https://infleqtion.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/Website_Backgrounds_1920x800px_MediumDark_tex_right.webp)
## Delivering Position, Navigation, and Timing (PNT) Without GPS
Infleqtion provides the “P and N” in PNT with its quantum inertial sensing solutions, enabling precise position and navigation in GPS-denied environments. Complementing this, [Infleqtion’s Tiqker atomic clock](https://infleqtion.com/tiqker/) technology delivers the “T” for timing, ensuring complete resilience in mission-critical scenarios.
Together, these technologies enable secure, accurate, and independent operation where traditional systems fail.
[Request a Demo](https://infleqtion.com/contact-sales/)
![Military plane in valley](https://infleqtion.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/Plane-in-Valley-1024x683.png)
![](https://infleqtion.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/Website_Backgrounds_1920x800px_Light_bg_tex_right.webp)
![chip-scale photonics for quantum sensing](https://infleqtion.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Chip_Scale_Photonics_v2-1024x585.png)
## Chip-Scale Photonics for Quantum Navigation
Quantum inertial sensing units rely on complex photonic systems to measure gravity, acceleration, and rotation. Chip-scale photonics allow these systems to shrink dramatically in size and cost, making them practical for assured navigation in GPS-denied environments. Infleqtion’s silicon photonics roadmap includes photonic-integrated circuit (PIC)-based inertial sensors that will set new standards in ruggedness and deployability.
[Learn More About Integrated Photonics](https://infleqtion.com/integrated-photonics/)

<h2 class="wp-block-heading" id="h-unprecedented-accuracy-reliable-performance-no-gps-required">Unprecedented Accuracy. Reliable Performance. No GPS Required.</h2>



<p>Infleqtion’s quantum inertial sensing technology is inherently stable and accurate over long time-scales because atoms are identical with unchanging properties. This almost eliminates bias errors and drift—critical limitations in conventional inertial sensors such as gravimeters, accelerometers, or gyroscopes.</p>



<ul class="wp-block-list">
<li>Ultra-high precision gravity mapping for national security and geo-exploration and earth monitoring</li>



<li>Long-duration GPS-independent navigation for aerospace and defense, providing resilience and autonomy</li>



<li>Absolute measurements reduce the need for calibration and re-calibration, improving up-time</li>
</ul>
</div>

<h2 class="wp-block-heading" id="h-delivering-position-navigation-and-timing-pnt-without-gps">Delivering Position, Navigation, and Timing (PNT) Without GPS</h2>



<p>Infleqtion provides the “P and N” in PNT with its quantum inertial sensing solutions, enabling precise position and navigation in GPS-denied environments. Complementing this, <a href="/tiqker/">Infleqtion’s Tiqker atomic clock</a> technology delivers the “T” for timing, ensuring complete resilience in mission-critical scenarios.</p>



<p>Together, these technologies enable secure, accurate, and independent operation where traditional systems fail.</p>

<h2 class="wp-block-heading" id="h-chip-scale-photonics-for-quantum-navigation">Chip-Scale Photonics for Quantum Navigation</h2>



<p>Quantum inertial sensing units rely on complex photonic systems to measure gravity, acceleration, and rotation. Chip-scale photonics allow these systems to shrink dramatically in size and cost, making them practical for assured navigation in GPS-denied environments. Infleqtion’s silicon photonics roadmap includes photonic-integrated circuit (PIC)-based inertial sensors that will set new standards in ruggedness and deployability.</p>

        <script type="text/plain">
            (function () {
                function loadIframe(el) {
                    if (!el || el.dataset.loaded) return;
                    el.dataset.loaded = "1";
                    var id = el.getAttribute('data-ytid');
                    var src = 'https://www.youtube.com/embed/' + encodeURIComponent(id) + '?autoplay=1&rel=0';
                    var ifr = document.createElement('iframe');
                    ifr.setAttribute('src', src);
                    ifr.setAttribute('title', 'YouTube video');
                    ifr.setAttribute('frameborder', '0');
                    ifr.setAttribute('allow', 'accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share');
                    ifr.setAttribute('allowfullscreen', '1');
                    ifr.setAttribute('loading', 'lazy');
                    ifr.style.width = '100%';
                    ifr.style.height = '100%';
                    el.innerHTML = '';
                    el.appendChild(ifr);
                }

                // Click to play
                document.addEventListener('click', function (e) {
                    var el = e.target.closest('.yt-lite');
                    if (el) loadIframe(el);
                });

                // Keyboard accessible
                document.addEventListener('keydown', function (e) {
                    var active = document.activeElement;
                    if (!active || !active.classList || !active.classList.contains('yt-lite')) return;
                    if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') {
                        e.preventDefault();
                        loadIframe(active);
                    }
                });

                if ('IntersectionObserver' in window) {
                    var io = new IntersectionObserver(function (entries) {
                        entries.forEach(function (entry) {
                            if (entry.isIntersecting) entry.target.dataset.prepared = "1";
                        });
                    }, { rootMargin: '200px 0px' });
                    document.querySelectorAll('.yt-lite').forEach(function (el) { io.observe(el); });
                }
            })();
        </script>
        <style>
            /* Works with existing .video-grid/.video-item styles */
            .yt-lite {
                position: relative;
                cursor: pointer;
                background: #000;
                overflow: hidden;
                border-radius: 6px;
            }

            .yt-lite::before {
                content: "";
                display: block;
                padding-bottom: 56.25%;
            }

            /* 16:9 */
            .yt-lite>* {
                position: absolute;
                inset: 0;
                width: 100%;
                height: 100%;
            }

            .yt-lite .yt-thumb {
                width: 100%;
                height: 100%;
                object-fit: cover;
            }

            .yt-lite .yt-play {
                position: absolute;
                left: 50%;
                top: 50%;
                transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
                width: 68px;
                height: 48px;
                background: rgba(0, 0, 0, .6);
                border-radius: 12px;
                box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(0, 0, 0, .35);
            }

            .yt-lite .yt-play::before {
                content: "";
                position: absolute;
                left: 50%;
                top: 50%;
                transform: translate(-40%, -50%);
                border-style: solid;
                border-width: 12px 0 12px 20px;
                border-color: transparent transparent transparent #fff;
            }

            .yt-lite:focus {
                outline: 2px solid #6aa9ff;
                outline-offset: 2px;
            }

            .video-item p {
                text-align: center;
                margin-top: 12px;
                margin-bottom: 0;
            }
        </style>
